beginner.question: How do you align text in CSS?
beginner.answer: Use the text-align property: text-align: left; (default), text-align: right;, text-align: center;, or text-align: justify;. text-align applies to block containers and aligns inline content within the block. For inline elements, use the parent's text-align property. text-align also centers inline-block elements when applied to the parent.

beginner.question: How do you add an underline, overline, or strikethrough to text in CSS?
beginner.answer: Use the text-decoration property: text-decoration: underline; (underline), text-decoration: overline; (line above), text-decoration: line-through; (strikethrough), or text-decoration: none; (remove default link underlines). Modern CSS also supports text-decoration-color, text-decoration-style (solid, double, dotted, dashed, wavy), and text-decoration-thickness.

beginner.question: How do you change text to uppercase or lowercase in CSS?
beginner.answer: Use the text-transform property: text-transform: uppercase; (ALL CAPS), text-transform: lowercase; (all lowercase), text-transform: capitalize; (First Letter Of Each Word), or text-transform: none; (default). text-transform only changes the visual appearance — the underlying HTML text remains unchanged.

beginner.question: How do you add a shadow to text in CSS?
beginner.answer: Use the text-shadow property: text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);. The syntax is: offset-x offset-y blur-radius color. The first two values are required (horizontal and vertical offset), while blur-radius and color are optional. You can also add multiple shadows by comma-separating them: text-shadow: 1px 1px 0 #000, 2px 2px 0 #333;

beginner.question: What does text-indent do in CSS?
beginner.answer: text-indent indents the first line of a block of text. It accepts length values (px, em, rem) or percentages (percentage of the containing block's width). Example: text-indent: 30px; indents the first line by 30 pixels. Negative values create a hanging indent. This is commonly used in print-like layouts for paragraphs.

beginner.question: How do you create ellipsis (...) for overflowing text in CSS?
beginner.answer: Use a combination of three properties: .truncate { white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; }. white-space: nowrap prevents wrapping, overflow: hidden clips the excess, and text-overflow: ellipsis adds the '...' to indicate truncated content. This works for single-line text only — multi-line truncation requires additional techniques.

beginner.question: How do you control the space between characters and words?
beginner.answer: Use letter-spacing for character spacing (letter-spacing: 2px;) and word-spacing for word spacing (word-spacing: 5px;). Both accept positive values (increasing space) or negative values (decreasing space). letter-spacing is useful for headings and uppercase text. word-spacing affects spaces between words but not at line breaks.

beginner.question: What is line-height and how does it affect readability?
beginner.answer: line-height controls the vertical space between lines of text. A unitless value (line-height: 1.5) is recommended — it's relative to the font-size. A line-height of 1.5-1.8 is considered optimal for body text readability. Too tight (1.0) makes lines crowded; too loose (2.5+) makes text disconnected. Unitless values are inherited as a ratio, so child elements maintain proportional spacing regardless of their font-size.

beginner.question: How do you remove the default underline from links?
beginner.answer: Use text-decoration: none; on anchor elements: a { text-decoration: none; }. Links have text-decoration: underline by default in all browsers. If you remove the underline, you must provide another visual indicator (like color difference, hover effect, or boldness) so users can still identify links, as per accessibility guidelines.

beginner.question: What does white-space: nowrap do in CSS?
beginner.answer: white-space: nowrap prevents text from wrapping to a new line. All text remains on a single line, potentially causing horizontal overflow. It's commonly used for: preventing line breaks in navigation items, keeping labels and values together on the same line, and as part of the text-overflow: ellipsis truncation technique.

intermediate.question: How does text-shadow work with multiple shadows and what visual effects can you create?
intermediate.answer: Multiple shadows are comma-separated, painted from front to back: text-shadow: 1px 1px 0 #000, 2px 2px 0 #333, 3px 3px 0 #666; creates a 3D raised text effect. You can also create: glow effects (no offset, colored blur), neon text (multiple layered shadows with increasing blur), outline text (four directional shadows with zero blur), and letterpress effects (light shadow above, dark shadow below).

intermediate.question: What is the difference between text-align: justify and text-align: left?
intermediate.answer: text-align: left aligns text to the left edge, leaving the right edge ragged (uneven). text-align: justify stretches each line (except the last) so both left and right edges are even by adding extra space between words. Justify can create large uneven gaps ('rivers') on narrow columns. It should be used with hyphenation (hyphens: auto) for best results.

intermediate.question: How do you handle long words or URLs that break layouts?
intermediate.answer: Use overflow-wrap (formerly word-wrap): overflow-wrap: break-word; forces long words to break at any character if they would overflow. word-break: break-all; breaks at every character boundary (more aggressive). word-break: break-word; is a legacy value that acts like overflow-wrap: break-word. For URLs specifically, adding a <wbr> (word break opportunity) or zero-width space (​) in the HTML gives you manual control.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between letter-spacing and word-spacing?
intermediate.answer: letter-spacing (tracking) adjusts space between individual characters: letter-spacing: 0.05em; adds 5% of the font-size between each character. word-spacing adjusts space between words only (the space character). letter-spacing affects the entire text uniformly; word-spacing affects inter-word gaps. letter-spacing is commonly used on headings for a design effect; word-spacing is rarely adjusted.

intermediate.question: How does text-decoration-thickness and text-underline-offset work?
intermediate.answer: text-decoration-thickness sets the thickness of the decoration line (underline, overline, line-through): text-decoration-thickness: 2px; or text-decoration-thickness: 0.1em;. text-underline-offset positions the underline relative to its default position: text-underline-offset: 3px; moves it 3px further from the text. Both are CSS Text Decoration Level 4 features that give fine-grained control over underline appearance.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between white-space: nowrap and white-space: pre?
intermediate.answer: white-space: nowrap collapses multiple spaces into one (normal whitespace handling) but prevents line breaks — text stays on one line. white-space: pre preserves all whitespace and line breaks exactly as in the HTML source (like a <pre> tag) — spaces, tabs, and newlines are all rendered. white-space: pre-wrap preserves whitespace but allows text to wrap normally.

intermediate.question: How do you set text direction for right-to-left languages like Arabic or Hebrew?
intermediate.answer: Use the direction property: direction: rtl; (right-to-left) or direction: ltr; (default). This changes the text baseline direction and the writing direction. For complete support, also use the unicode-bidi property for proper Unicode bidirectional text handling. The HTML dir attribute (dir="rtl") is also effective and recommended over CSS for semantic content.

intermediate.question: How does text-transform affect accessibility and screen readers?
intermediate.answer: text-transform: uppercase changes the visual appearance but screen readers generally read the original HTML text, not the transformed version. However, some screen readers may read all-caps text letter-by-letter (as acronyms), causing confusion. For visually-uppercased text that should not be read as acronyms, use text-transform rather than writing uppercase in HTML. For actual acronyms, use <abbr> with proper title attribute.

intermediate.question: What does the tab-size property do in CSS?
intermediate.answer: tab-size controls the width of tab characters (\t) in pre-formatted text or elements with white-space: pre. The default is 8 (8 spaces wide). You can set it to a specific number: tab-size: 4; or to a length: tab-size: 2em;. This affects <pre> tags, <code> blocks, and any element that preserves whitespace.

intermediate.question: How do vertical-align values like super, sub, top, middle work?
intermediate.answer: vertical-align controls the vertical positioning of inline/inline-block elements relative to the line box. Values: baseline (default — aligns to text baseline), top (to top of line box), bottom (to bottom of line box), middle (to middle of parent's x-height + half ascender), super (superscript position), sub (subscript position), text-top (to top of parent's text), text-bottom (to bottom of parent's text), and length/percentage values (raise/lower from baseline).

advanced.question: Explain the complete line box model and how line-height, font-size, and vertical-align determine the final line box height.
advanced.answer: The line box height is determined by the highest element on the line, calculated as follows: each inline element contributes a 'strut' (a zero-width character with the parent's font-size and line-height). The line box height = max(ascender + descender) of all elements on the line, where ascender and descender come from each element's font metrics combined with its vertical-align offset. Line-height: 1.5 means the line box height is 1.5x the current font-size, split into half-leading above and below the font's ascender-descender span.

advanced.question: How does the CSS text rendering pipeline handle OpenType features like ligatures, kerning, and alternates?
advanced.answer: CSS provides font-feature-settings and font-variant-* properties to control OpenType features. font-kerning controls kerning (space between specific character pairs). font-variant-ligatures controls common/discretionary ligatures (fi, fl, ffi). font-variant-numeric controls figure styles (lining/tabular/oldstyle). font-variant-east-asian controls CJK-specific variants. Modern browsers also support font-variant-alternates for stylistic alternates. These properties expose the font's built-in typographic capabilities.

advanced.question: How do you implement multi-line text truncation with an ellipsis (...), and what are the limitations?
advanced.answer: CSS natively only supports single-line truncation. For multi-line truncation with ellipsis, use the -webkit-line-clamp approach: .clamp { display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 3; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; overflow: hidden; }. This is a non-standard but widely supported technique. Limitations: (1) Only works in WebKit/Blink browsers (Chrome, Safari, Edge); (2) The -webkit-box display is a deprecated flexbox implementation; (3) Firefox doesn't support line-clamp; (4) Requires a container with explicit or implicit height.

advanced.question: Explain how text-decoration-skip-ink works and why it matters for readability.
advanced.answer: text-decoration-skip-ink (default: auto) makes underlines skip over descenders — the parts of letters like g, j, p, q, y that extend below the baseline. When enabled, the underline breaks (or thins) where it would intersect the descender, making text more readable. When disabled (text-decoration-skip-ink: none), the underline cuts through descenders, which can make letters harder to read. This is particularly important for text with underlines in small font sizes.

advanced.question: How does the CSS text rendering differ for system fonts vs web fonts vs Google Fonts?
advanced.answer: System fonts are already installed and render immediately with native font rendering engines (ClearType on Windows, Core Text on macOS, FreeType on Linux). Web fonts (@font-face) require downloading and may cause FOIT (Flash of Invisible Text) or FOUT (Flash of Unstyled Text) depending on font-display settings. Google Fonts are a type of web font loaded via external CSS — they add DNS lookup, SSL negotiation, and download time. Text rendering also varies by OS: macOS typically renders fonts with more weight, Windows with sharper contrast, and results can differ significantly.

advanced.question: How do you handle text overflow in a way that respects both single-line and multi-line scenarios?
advanced.answer: A comprehensive text overflow strategy includes: (1) Single-line: white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; (2) Multi-line: -webkit-line-clamp (as above); (3) For flexible containers: use min-width: 0 on flex items to allow truncation; (4) Use overflow-wrap: break-word for long words; (5) For scrollable text, use overflow: auto and reserve scrollbar space with scrollbar-gutter; (6) Consider using data-* attributes and CSS content for showing tooltip with full text on hover.

advanced.question: How does the CSS spec define the 'hyphens' property and its interaction with different languages?
advanced.answer: The hyphens property controls hyphenation at line breaks: none (never hyphenate), manual (only at ­ or ‐), or auto (browser determines hyphenation points). auto requires the browser to have hyphenation dictionaries for the specified language (set via lang attribute). Not all languages have built-in hyphenation dictionaries in all browsers. Hyphenation is language-specific — hyphenation rules differ significantly between English, German, French, and other languages.

advanced.question: What factors determine the visual font size beyond the CSS font-size value?
advanced.answer: The visual font size depends on several factors beyond font-size: (1) The font's own metrics — different fonts at 16px can appear different sizes; (2) The font's x-height — fonts with larger x-heights appear larger at the same size; (3) Rendering engine differences — macOS renders more boldly than Windows; (4) Anti-aliasing and subpixel rendering; (5) Device pixel ratio (Retina vs standard); (6) Browser zoom level (which is a visual scaling of CSS pixels); (7) OS-level font size settings.

advanced.question: How do you use CSS to create a drop cap (initial letter) effect?
advanced.answer: Use the ::first-letter pseudo-element with float and font-size: .article p:first-of-type::first-letter { font-size: 3em; float: left; line-height: 0.8; margin-right: 8px; }. CSS also has the experimental initial-letter property: initial-letter: 3; (takes 3 lines). However, initial-letter has limited browser support, so the float technique is more widely used. The ::first-letter pseudo-element only works on block containers and picks the first typographic letter.

advanced.question: Explain how the CSS writing-mode and text-orientation properties work for vertical text layouts.
advanced.answer: writing-mode: vertical-rl makes text flow vertically from right to left (used for traditional Japanese, Chinese, Korean). vertical-lr flows vertically left to right. text-orientation controls character orientation in vertical mode: mixed (default — CJK upright, Latin rotated 90°), upright (all characters upright), sideways (all characters rotated 90° sideways). When using vertical text, logical properties (margin-block, padding-inline) become especially important as the axes swap.
